result.stdout:
Description:
  todo is a .NET command line port of the Todo.txt shell script from http://todotxt.org/ that tries to remain faithful to the command line and functionality of the original shell script wherever possible. Written by Rob Prouse.

Usage:
  todo [command] [options]

Options:
  -a              Don't auto-archive tasks automatically on completion
  -d <d>          Use a configuration file other than the default ~/.todo.json
  -f              Forces actions without confirmation or interactive input.
  -p              Plain mode turns off colors
  -t              Prepend the current date to a task automatically when it's added.
  --version       Show version information
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  a, add <task>                      Adds THING I NEED TO DO to your todo.txt file on its own line.
  addm <tasks>                       Adds "FIRST THING I NEED TO DO" to your todo.txt on its own line and adds "SECOND THING I NEED TO DO" to you todo.txt on its own line.
  addto <filename> <task>            Adds a line of text to any file located in the todo.txt directory.
  app, append <item> <text>          Adds TEXT TO APPEND to the end of the task on line ITEM#.
  archive                            Moves all done tasks from todo.txt to done.txt and removes blank lines.
  delete, rm <item> <term>           Deletes the task on line ITEM# in todo.txt. If TERM specifiedeletes only TERM from the task. []
  depri, dp <items>                  Deprioritizes (removes the priority) from the task(s) on line ITEM# in todo.txt. []
  edit                               Opens the folder containing the todo.txt file in VS Code.
  do <items>                         Marks task(s) on line ITEM# as done in todo.txt. []
  list, ls <terms>                   Displays all tasks that contain TERM(s) sorted by priority with line numbers. Each task must match all TERM(s) (logical AND). Hides all tasks that contain TERM(s) preceded by a minus sign (i.e. -TERM). []
  listall, lsa <terms>               Displays all the lines in todo.txt AND done.txt that contain TERM(s) sorted by priority with line numbers. Hides all tasks that contain TERM(s) preceded by a minus sign(i.e. -TERM). If no TERM specified, lists entire todo.txt AND done.txt concatenated and sorted. []
  listcon, lsc <terms>               Lists all the task contexts that start with the @ sign in todo.txt. If TERM specified, considers only tasks that contain TERM(s). []
  lf, listfile <filename> <terms>    Displays all tasks that contain TERM(s) sorted by priority with line numbers. Each task must match all TERM(s) (logical AND). Hides all tasks that contain TERM(s) preceded by a minus sign (i.e. -TERM). []
  listpri, lsp <priorities> <terms>  Displays all tasks prioritized PRIORITIES. PRIORITIES can be a single one (A) or a range (A-C). If no PRIORITIES specified, lists all prioritized tasks. If TERM specified, lists only prioritized tasks that contain TERM(s). Hides all tasks that contain TERM(s) preceded by a minus sign(i.e. -TERM). [priorities: A-Z, ]
  listproj, lspj <terms>             Lists all the projects (terms that start with a + sign) in todo.txt. If TERM specified, considers only tasks that contain TERM(s). []
  prep, prepend <item> <text>        Adds TEXT TO PREPEND to the beginning of the task on line ITEM#.
  p, pri <item> <priority>           Adds PRIORITY to task on line ITEM#. If the task is already prioritized, replaces current priority with new PRIORITY. PRIORITY must be a letter between A and Z.
  replace <item> <text>              Replaces task on line ITEM# with UPDATED TODO.

commands.payload:
Description:
  todo is a .NET command line port of the Todo.txt shell script from http://todotxt.org/ that tries to remain faithful to the command line and functionality of the original shell script wherever possible. Written by Rob Prouse.

Usage:
  todo [command] [options]

Options:
  -a              Don't auto-archive tasks automatically on completion
  -d <d>          Use a configuration file other than the default ~/.todo.json
  -f              Forces actions without confirmation or interactive input.
  -p              Plain mode turns off colors
  -t              Prepend the current date to a task automatically when it's added.
  --version       Show version information
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  a, add <task>                      Adds THING I NEED TO DO to your todo.txt file on its own line.
  addm <tasks>                       Adds "FIRST THING I NEED TO DO" to your todo.txt on its own line and adds "SECOND THING I NEED TO DO" to you todo.txt on its own line.
  addto <filename> <task>            Adds a line of text to any file located in the todo.txt directory.
  app, append <item> <text>          Adds TEXT TO APPEND to the end of the task on line ITEM#.
  archive                            Moves all done tasks from todo.txt to done.txt and removes blank lines.
  delete, rm <item> <term>           Deletes the task on line ITEM# in todo.txt. If TERM specifiedeletes only TERM from the task. []
  depri, dp <items>                  Deprioritizes (removes the priority) from the task(s) on line ITEM# in todo.txt. []
  edit                               Opens the folder containing the todo.txt file in VS Code.
  do <items>                         Marks task(s) on line ITEM# as done in todo.txt. []
  list, ls <terms>                   Displays all tasks that contain TERM(s) sorted by priority with line numbers. Each task must match all TERM(s) (logical AND). Hides all tasks that contain TERM(s) preceded by a minus sign (i.e. -TERM). []
  listall, lsa <terms>               Displays all the lines in todo.txt AND done.txt that contain TERM(s) sorted by priority with line numbers. Hides all tasks that contain TERM(s) preceded by a minus sign(i.e. -TERM). If no TERM specified, lists entire todo.txt AND done.txt concatenated and sorted. []
  listcon, lsc <terms>               Lists all the task contexts that start with the @ sign in todo.txt. If TERM specified, considers only tasks that contain TERM(s). []
  lf, listfile <filename> <terms>    Displays all tasks that contain TERM(s) sorted by priority with line numbers. Each task must match all TERM(s) (logical AND). Hides all tasks that contain TERM(s) preceded by a minus sign (i.e. -TERM). []
  listpri, lsp <priorities> <terms>  Displays all tasks prioritized PRIORITIES. PRIORITIES can be a single one (A) or a range (A-C). If no PRIORITIES specified, lists all prioritized tasks. If TERM specified, lists only prioritized tasks that contain TERM(s). Hides all tasks that contain TERM(s) preceded by a minus sign(i.e. -TERM). [priorities: A-Z, ]
  listproj, lspj <terms>             Lists all the projects (terms that start with a + sign) in todo.txt. If TERM specified, considers only tasks that contain TERM(s). []
  prep, prepend <item> <text>        Adds TEXT TO PREPEND to the beginning of the task on line ITEM#.
  p, pri <item> <priority>           Adds PRIORITY to task on line ITEM#. If the task is already prioritized, replaces current priority with new PRIORITY. PRIORITY must be a letter between A and Z.
  replace <item> <text>              Replaces task on line ITEM# with UPDATED TODO.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Displays all tasks prioritized PRIORITIES. PRIORITIES can be a single one (A) or a range (A-C). If no PRIORITIES specified, lists all prioritized tasks. If TERM specified, lists only prioritized tasks that contain TERM(s). Hides all tasks that contain TERM(s) preceded by a minus sign(i.e. -TERM).

Usage:
  todo listpri [<priorities> [<terms>...]] [options]

Arguments:
  <priorities>  [default: A-Z]
  <terms>       []

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
